title: Smoky Syrian Kebabs
description: A flavorful and aromatic dish inspired by the Middle East, featuring smoky Syrian kebabs made with smoky, sweet, and syrian spices, served with a sweet beet chutney.
instructions: In a small bowl, mix together smoky paprika, cumin, coriander powder, garlic powder, salt, and black pepper.
Add the spice mix to the kebab meat and mix well.
Thread the meat onto skewers, leaving a little space between each piece.
Brush the grill with olive oil to prevent sticking.
Grill the kebabs for 8-10 minutes, turning occasionally, until cooked through.
While the kebabs are cooking, roast the beets in the oven at 400°F (200°C) for 45 minutes, or until tender.
Once the beets are cool enough to handle, peel and chop them into a fine paste.
In a small saucepan, combine the beet paste, brown sugar, and apple cider vinegar.
Bring the mixture to a simmer over medium heat and cook for 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the chutney has thickened.
Serve the kebabs with the sweet beet chutney.
